title: Differences in Community Recognition of Autism Between Special Education and Clinicians: Insights from Public Health Surveillance in Arizona
authors_full: Joshua Anbar, DrPH¹; Amber Catalan, BS¹,*; Tyra Bozek, MS¹; Jennifer Andrews, PhD²

summary.hook: This poster compares how autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is “recognized” across education (autism special education exceptionality) versus clinical systems (ASD ICD codes /...
summary.summary_30s: This poster compares how autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is “recognized” across education (autism special education exceptionality) versus clinical systems (ASD ICD codes / diagnostic statements) using Arizona ADDM surveillance data on 8-year-olds from 2018/2020/2022 (n=873).

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster compares how autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is “recognized” across education (autism special education exceptionality) versus clinical systems (ASD ICD codes...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Observational surveillance analysis (secondary analysis of public health surveillance data). Overall n=873; record source subgroups: Single Source n=529, Multiple Source n=344
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: Recognition categories show limited overlap between educational and clinical systems (n=873): 48% education-only, 18% ICD-only, 29% both, 6% neither.
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: Multiple record sources increase likelihood of recognition by both systems.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: In Arizona ADDM, what specific sources qualify as a “record source,” and how do you classify “single” vs “multiple” sources?

key_numbers: Overall n=873; record source subgroups: Single Source n=529, Multiple Source n=344
8-year-old children in Arizona ADDM surveillance cycles
Recognition categories: 48% education-only, 18% ICD-only, 29% both, 6% neither (n=873).
Multiple sources: OR=1.77 for exceptionality (Holm p=0.002).
takeaways: Recognition categories show limited overlap between educational and clinical systems (n=873): 48% education-only, 18% ICD-only, 29% both, 6% neither.
Among children with multiple sources, agreement between exceptionality and ICD code is near zero: Cohen’s κ \= -0.001
Multinomial regression (statistically significant predictors shown in Figure 1) reports relative risk ratios by category for: race/ethnicity, intellectual disability...

grad: In Arizona ADDM, what specific sources qualify as a “record source,” and how do you classify “single” vs “multiple” sources?

researcher: In Arizona ADDM, what specific sources qualify as a “record source,” and how do you classify “single” vs “multiple” sources?
In your four-category overlap plot, are categories based strictly on special education exceptionality vs ICD code, or do clinical diagnostic statements alter category assignment?
What is your operational definition of “autism special education exceptionality” (what documentation in education records triggers it)?
